primary_method.name: ExitMultiple
primary_method.reasoning: AEVA is a single-segment highgrowth_preprofit company. The FMCW LiDAR platform — automotive, industrial, CityOS — shares one risk profile and one growth driver (production ramp). No separate annuity or cyclical segment exists today; the industrial and CityOS sub-lines are early-stage extensions of the same technology, not independent businesses. ExitMultiple is the correct method per CONTRACT §2.2 and the registry for highgrowth_preprofit: normalize to FY2033 (T=7 from end of FY2026, when contracted program volumes have ramped and growth decelerates to high single digits), apply the steady-state Gordon multiple, discount at segment WACC.

segments.name: FMCW LiDAR Platform (Automotive + Industrial + CityOS)
segments.reasoning: Single-segment exit-multiple on NOPAT path to FY2033 normalization. NOPAT = EBIT x (1 - effective_cash_tax_rate). NOLs will shelter taxes well into the early-normalization years; effective cash tax ~5% through FY2030, rising to ~18% by FY2033 as NOLs are consumed. EBIT = EBITDA - D&A (~$12M at normalization). Base: FY2033 revenue $290M, EBITDA margin 43%, D&A $12M, EBIT $112.7M, NOPAT $89M. WACC 11.0% (fundamental beta 1.3 — credits contracted program de-risking vs. observed beta 2.41, per CONTRACT §2.3). g_lt 4.5% (hardware/software platform growing with auto production + software mix). ROIC 28% (capital-light design: manufacturing partner carries capex; IP/software is high-ROIC; consistent with Aeva's asset-light model). Gordon multiple = (1 - 0.045/0.28) / (0.11 - 0.045) = 0.839/0.065 = 12.91x EV/NOPAT.

exit_inputs.reasoning: nopat_path derived from demand.json base revenue x margin: FY2026-FY2029 losses as ramp costs exceed revenue; FY2030 breakeven; FY2031-2033 growing NOPAT. interim_fcf_pv_b = -$180M representing the PV of cumulative negative FCF years FY2026-FY2030 at 11% WACC (these are CASH OUTFLOWS that the equity holder funds; they reduce equity value today). Net_debt_b $0.5M (Q1 2026: $99.5M cash minus $100M Apollo convertible). Shares 73.8M includes 6.3M dilutive shares from Apollo convert at $15.86 (in-the-money at current $24.39).

earnings_bridge.notes: AEVA is pre-profitability. FY2025 FCF -$120M / 67.5M basic shares = -$1.78/share. SBC estimated ~$40M annualized (typical for 239-person tech hardware company); net dilution after open-market buybacks (none reported) is the full SBC dilution. Primary metric: EV/Revenue and path to FCF. Street consensus EPS NTM -$1.54 is consistent with the FY2026E guided revenue of $30-36M and ~$120M operating cost base.

scenarios.label: Bear
scenarios.probability_reasoning: No probability assigned per CONTRACT — range, not a blend
scenarios.reasoning: SOP delays 12-18 months. Daimler Truck slips to Q4 2028; EU OEM slips to 2030. NRE stays flat; industrial scales slowly. Forced dilutive capital raise in 2028 at ~$12/share (+12.5M shares -> 80M diluted). FY2033 revenue $185M, EBITDA margin 30%, NOPAT $34M.

scenarios.label: Bull
scenarios.probability_reasoning: No probability assigned per CONTRACT — range, not a blend
scenarios.reasoning: Contracted programs on schedule. 2 Luminar-replacement wins close in 2026-2027. NVIDIA Hyperion pull-through adds a 3rd OEM by 2028. Industrial and CityOS scale materially. FY2033 revenue $440M, EBITDA 52%, NOPAT $167M.
